problem_53.natural_language: A uniform limit of Lipschitz functions with constant `L` is Lipschitz with the same constant.
problem_53.lean:
theorem lipschitzWith_of_tendsto_uniformly
    {X : Type*} [PseudoMetricSpace X]
    (f_n : Nat → X → Real) (f : X → Real) {L : NNReal}
    (hL : ∀ n, LipschitzWith L (f_n n))
    (hconv : TendstoUniformly (fun n x => f_n n x) f atTop) :
    LipschitzWith L f
problem_53.natural_language_proof:
Proof: fix `x,y`. For any `n`,
`|f x - f y| ≤ |f x - f_n n x| + |f_n n x - f_n n y| + |f_n n y - f y|`.
The middle term is at most `L * dist x y` since `f_n n` is `L`-Lipschitz. By uniform convergence,
we can make the first and third terms uniformly as small as we like by taking `n` large. Letting
this error go to `0` yields `|f x - f y| ≤ L * dist x y` for all `x,y`, so `f` is Lipschitz with
constant `L`.
